event.intro: The Event of the Month picks a country, continent or theme each month to create and improve LGBT+ content on Wikipedia. Hover or tap a country on the map to see its editions, or browse the themed events in the list.
event.mapAria: World map of the Event of the Month countries
event.prompt: Hover or tap a highlighted country to see its data.
event.prizeArticles: Award for creating the most articles
event.prizeLesbian: Award for creating the most lesbian biographies

footer.credit: Made with ❤️ by Nacaru with the contribution of the rest of the Wikiproyecto LGBT+

visibilize.title: Make it visible
visibilize.text: Bring diversity to the largest encyclopedia

stats.intro1: The LGBT+ WikiProject was founded on the Spanish Wikipedia in 2007. We have had periods of greater and lesser activity, but overall many articles have been worked on throughout this time.
stats.intro2: Using the top menu, you can browse through the different data gathered from the work of the people who make up the project. They are live figures, so don't be surprised if you see them change from one moment to the next!
stats.pickSection: Pick one of the sections in the menu above

memberCreations.empty: No LGBT articles created by this user.

modal.byContent: Share of the article's current content, by characters (XTools · WikiWho data).
modal.creator: Created by

articles.text1: Although there are many different ways to contribute as part of WMLGBT+, articles are the most recognizable feature of Wikipedia. In total, our community has created:
articles.text2: In the chart you can see how well we've done over the last few years

monthly.text1: Events such as the Challenge of the Month help us keep our monthly edits high. So far this month we have created:
monthly.text2: Our busiest periods tend to be concentrated in March and June, can you guess why?

participants.text1: The cornerstone of our community is its participants. Right now we are:
participants.text2: In the chart you can see the new sign-ups over the last few years

notable.text1: Featured articles and good articles represent the highest level of quality within Wikipedia.
notable.text2: At the LGBT+ WikiProject we are aware of this, so we have worked over the years to bring many of the pages we build up to this level.

lastArticles.noImage: This article does not have an image, you can add one
